ai:cl.c: System processes are managed by the Task class.

ai:a.c: Activating a task object will execute it.

ai:i.c: The name of a key value.
Pointer to a buffer space large enough to hold the retrieved value.

ai:a.c: Reads executable metadata from the file referenced in Location.
Variable fields are supported for the general storage of program variables.
ai:a.p: ERR acSetKey(*Object, std::string_view Key, std::string_view Value)

ai:i.c: The name of the target key.
The string value to associate with Key.

ai:a.c: Send raw data to a launched process' stdin descriptor.
ai:a.p: ERR acWrite(*Object, std::span<const int8_t> Buffer, INT *Result)

ai:i.c: A buffer containing the data that will be written to the object.
This parameter with be updated with the total number of bytes written from the Buffer.

ai:m.c: Adds a new argument to the Parameters field.
Forces a Task to expunge unused code.
Retrieves environment variables for the active process.
ai:m.p: ERR task::GetEnv(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Name, STRING * Value)

ai:i.c: The name of the environment variable to retrieve.
The value of the environment variable is returned in this parameter.

ai:m.c: Sends a quit message to a task.
Sets environment variables for the active process.
ai:m.p: ERR task::SetEnv(OBJECTPTR Object, STRVIEW Name, STRVIEW Value)

ai:i.c: The name of the environment variable to set.
The value to assign to the environment variable. If empty, the variable is removed.

ai:f.c: Used to gain direct access to a task's actions.
Controls which CPU cores the process can run on.
This callback returns incoming data from STDERR.
The callback is activated when the process is terminated.
This callback returns incoming data from STDIN.
Returns a list of all key names available to the GetKeys() action.
Launched executables will start in the path specified here.
Location of an executable file to launch.
This callback returns incoming data from STDOUT.
The current working folder of the active process.
The task priority in relation to other tasks is be defined here.
Reflects the process ID when an executable is launched.
The path of the executable that is associated with the task.
The task's return code can be retrieved following execution.
Limits the amount of time to wait for a launched process to return.
